title: Apple made a movie trailer vibrate in sync with an F1 car. I tried to do the same thing in a browser.
excerpt: The Web Vibration API exists. Most developers don't know about it. Here's what happens when you actually push it — and where it hits a wall called iPhone.

title: Jhey posts CSS gold on Twitter every week. I built a database so none of it gets lost.
excerpt: 5,698 tweets. 1,701 curated tips. One question: why doesn't this exist already?

title: Pasting Clipboard Images into Remote AI Coding Agents over SSH
excerpt: How I wired up Ctrl+V in WezTerm to transparently deliver clipboard images to Claude Code and OpenCode running on a remote server: no display server, no second connection, no third-party tools.

title: I spent a weekend studying Marc Lou & Jack Friks' landing pages. Here's what I found.
excerpt: Two indie hackers. Four products. Nearly identical page structures. This is what I learned about how successful products actually sell themselves.
